nameMeanings.name: Dimick
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (Middlesex): variant of   Dimock  . This form of the surname is now rare in Britain.
Americanized form of Serbian Dimić: patronymic from a short form of the personal name Dimitrije, from Greek Dēmētrios (see Dimitris ).
Americanized form of Slovenian Dimic: nickname for a gray-haired or gray-bearded man, from dimec ‘graybeard’.

variants.name: Dimock
meanings: English (Cambridgeshire): habitational name from Dymock in Gloucestershire, named perhaps from a British word akin to Welsh tymoch ‘pigsty’, but more probably from a Celtic din ‘fort’ + moch ‘swine’. A family of the name Dymoke held the hereditary position of King's Champion for thirty-four generations, starting in the late 14th century when John Dymoke adopted the role following his marriage to Margaret de Ludlow.

nameMeanings.name: or
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Jewish (Israeli): from Hebrew or ‘light’, a Hebraicized form of various original Ashkenazic surnames.
Chinese: Cantonese form of the surname 柯, see   Ke   1.
Chinese: Hokkien form of the surname 胡, see   Hu   1.

variants.name: Ke
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Heng, Yong, Bin, Song, Yan, Zhi, Cheng, Chih-Ming, Chun-Yen, Fan, Fong, Gang.  Vietnamese Lan, Hai, Hoa, Le Van, Mui, Tuy.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 柯, meaning ‘bough, stalk’ in Chinese: (i) from the first element of the personal name Ke Lu (柯盧), personal name of a prince of the state of Wu during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ). (ii) from the first element of the placename Ke Ze (柯澤), the name of a fief (located in Shandong province) granted to an official in the state of Qi during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ). (iii) traced back to the Qiang ethnic group in ancient western China and the Xianbei ethnic group in northern China. (iv) adopted as a surname by the Ke Ba (柯拔) family from the Xianbei ethnic group in northern China during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ).

nameMeanings.name: Dimmoch
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: habitational name from Dymock in Gloucestershire; see   Dimock  .

variants.name: Dimock
meanings: English (Cambridgeshire): habitational name from Dymock in Gloucestershire, named perhaps from a British word akin to Welsh tymoch ‘pigsty’, but more probably from a Celtic din ‘fort’ + moch ‘swine’. A family of the name Dymoke held the hereditary position of King's Champion for thirty-four generations, starting in the late 14th century when John Dymoke adopted the role following his marriage to Margaret de Ludlow.

nameMeanings.name: Nicholas
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: form of the post-classical Greek personal name Nikolaos, derived from nikē ‘victory’ + laos ‘people’. The spelling with -ch- first occurred as early as the 12th century, and became firmly established at the time of the Reformation, although Nicolas is still occasionally found. St Nicholas was a 4th-century bishop of Myra in Lycia, about whom virtually nothing factual is known, although a vast body of legend grew up around him, and he became the patron saint of Greece and of Russia, as well as of children, sailors, merchants, and pawnbrokers. His feast day is 6 December , and among the many roles which legend has assigned to him is that of bringer of Christmas presents, in the guise of ‘Santa Claus’ (an alteration of the Dutch form of his name, Sinterklaas).

variants.name: Nickolas
meanings: Mainly U.S.: variant spelling of   Nicholas  , influenced by the short form   Nick .

en.lifeSummary: Nicholas Dimick or Dimmoch was born in 1120, in Dymock, Gloucestershire, England, United Kingdom. He married wife Lady Nicolas Dimick  or Dymmock I x. They were the parents of at least 1 son. He died in his hometown.

fr.lifeSummary: Nicholas Dimick or Dimmoch est né en 1120, à Dymock, Gloucestershire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Il a épousé wife Lady Nicolas Dimick  or Dymmock I x. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils. Il est décédé dans sa ville natale.

de.lifeSummary: Nicholas Dimick or Dimmoch wurde 1120 in Dymock, Gloucestershire, England, United Kingdom geboren. Er hat wife Lady Nicolas Dimick  or Dymmock I x geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn. Er starb in seinem Geburtsort.

it.lifeSummary: Nicholas Dimick or Dimmoch è nato il nel 1120 nel Dymock, Gloucestershire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Lui sposato wife Lady Nicolas Dimick  or Dymmock I x. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlio. Morì nel sua cittadina.

ko.lifeSummary: Nicholas Dimick or Dimmoch(은)는 1120년 영국 잉글랜드 그루스스터실르 디모크에서 태어났다. 그는 wife Lady Nicolas Dimick  or Dymmock I x(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 그의 고향에서 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Nicholas Dimick or Dimmoch nasceu em 1120, em Dymock, Gloucestershire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Ele casou-se com wife Lady Nicolas Dimick  or Dymmock I x. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho. Ele faleceu em sua cidade natal.

ru.lifeSummary: Nicholas Dimick or Dimmoch родился в 1120 г. в местности димокк, глоусестиршир, Англия, Великобритания. Он женился на женщине по имени wife Lady Nicolas Dimick  or Dymmock I x. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын. Он умер в его родном городе.

es.lifeSummary: Nicholas Dimick or Dimmoch nació en 1120, en Dymock, Gloucestershire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Se casó con wife Lady Nicolas Dimick  or Dymmock I x. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hijo. Murió en su pueblo.
